All right, welcome to the scoreboard. This thing is awesome and I love using it and it really helps keep your trainers motivated and it makes sure that everybody, it creates competition, you know? And if this is something that you should post, whether you have like a group voxer or you send out emails or text messages, whatever, this is something that I always post every time it gets updated with the new sale, minimum weekly, right? So let's go over what the scoreboard is and how it's used. So it is a simple spreadsheet. As you can see, that has your staff on here. Anybody that would be doing sales, sessions, whatever it may be, I like to put my entire staff on here even if they are doing nothing because it shows that you guys are a team, right? And as a team, you have certain goals that you wanna reach. Now, I have this broken up between sessions, which is just how many I like to make sure that my trainers are servicing a lot of sessions, which encourages them to, we do a lot of HTTP and one-on-one. And so I want them to get their own clients from the bootcamp. So we spend a lot of time and energy and getting everybody into our six-week challenge and they are loving it, they're crushing the challenge, we have 80 people at a time doing our challenge. I like my trainers to pick people out that are maybe struggling or they think could use an extra help or whatever it may be and send them to a higher level of service. And when they ascend them, that becomes an opportunity and a sale. So the opportunity is going to be them just sitting down and talking with somebody about perhaps starting to do one-on-one training or doing HTTP or doing online training, whatever makes sense for that person, just sitting down and having that conversation. So we schedule that person in acuity to make sure or you can do it right in your CRM like in mind body. And so then you know it's a real, it's an actual appointment that was serviced. It's not just the trainer saying, yeah, I talked with Bob the other day, it's a real appointment that you can verify happened. So my trainers have a goal of having two opportunities per month. Actually, this is a little bit outdated, we do four now, so once a week, I'd like them to sit down and talk with somebody. You can change this to whatever you think is appropriate. These are sales pitches to people that they think that should be ascended to a higher level of service, which is basically everybody, okay? So we track that and every time somebody does a session, we put a one there, everybody starts off with zero. Then if a sale happens, so if they sell a high ticket program for $29.99, maybe they sell a weekly program for, I don't know, $199 a week, whatever your stuff is, you can track here you put down their sales. Now, of course, you guys can set whatever goals you want, whatever makes sense for you at your gym, depending on what you guys normally sell. This is just the tracking system. So they had one opportunity, they made a sale, and so that results in more sessions. So now they have to service these sessions, right? They have to service these one-on-ones. So this is a combination of group classes, one-on-one sessions of online training, if you're doing that, HTTP. This is all of the sessions that they're servicing for the month. So I give them a goal of how many I would like them to service each month, and then I tally it up as the month goes on, and I add to it, add to it, add to it. Now, their goal is to get to 100% overall. If they hit 100%, I give them $100 bonus, right? If they hit 103%, they get $103 bonus. If they hit 114%, they get $114 bonus. If they get 99%, they get nothing, right? You wanna be 100%, you wanna be above and beyond. I pay them really well for doing their job, right? So if they're at 99%, that's just doing their job. Going above and beyond, I like to give a bonus for. I like to give additional compensation for them doing additional work, right? So a couple other categories here, you'll see standards. So I have a handbook, and I like everybody to maintain our standards. We have high standards at our gym, probably like you guys do, so never being late for sessions, not putting profanity on the radio, not eating while they're training, wearing their uniforms, things that I consider to be high standards, and we wanna be very professional. Anytime they violate one of my standards, they lose 25 points, or if it's a big violation, they can go straight to zero. I'm not giving you a bonus, because you did that, and that was really irresponsible. If you're not getting a bonus at all, you get zero points for standard. You can use your own judgment, right? But this is just the tracking system. And then I give them a trainer task, right? So they have to do a trainer task every month, and it might be something that's gonna be that I need from them, that I can't do myself. Like maybe getting some client testimonials of a one-on-one client they've been working with that can write something really cool and give some before and after pictures. Maybe their trainer task is making announcements before and after the class about a special event that we're doing, which they should be doing anyhow, so I wouldn't really use that as a task. Handwritten cards are the same thing. They should be doing those anyhow. So these are gonna be things that you need help with as an owner that you can't really do yourself. And then you give them an incentive for doing them. You're not just asking them to do stuff that isn't in there for hours, so they can reach a bonus, right? Now, this 103% is all of these, one, two, three, four categories added up, one, two, three, four, sorry, five categories added up. And you can see divided by five, so it takes the average of them. Why is this formula so long and looks so crazy? Because it's weighted, right? It's an if-then formula, so if you can see that, basically it says if the goal over the actual amount serviced is greater than two, then it equals two. All that means is that it can't go above 200%. So like as an example, if this sale is $3,000, then it will go up to 107, but it can't go higher than that $4,000. It's not gonna go any higher, because otherwise what you could have is, you could have one trainer that ends up selling a $5,000 paid-in-full package, right? And that immediately gives them like 500%, which means they don't even have to do any of the rest of these. Like just, and you could end up with a, and this happened to me, which is why I waited it, is I had a shitty trainer that was great at sales. And so they would bonus every month, and that was not setting a good example for the rest of my team. Like they would come in and not wear their uniform and stuff like that and be like, what, I get bonuses every month? Like I had 110% every month, you know? And it's like, just because you're great at sales, right? So we ended up waiting this, so none of these categories can exceed 200%, but it's mostly for sales. So that way it doesn't sway the overall percentage. We want really well-rounded trainers. So basically what you do is anytime there's a new opportunity, a new sale, I like to update the sessions every Monday, or anytime there's a violation in the standard, you update that right away so you don't forget, and it will update your overall percentages. Like I wrote up here, don't change the formulas, because we're not, don't change these formulas, okay? Just let them be. And so it will, at the end, tell you if you hit your goal as a gym, and part of what I do is at our monthly meetings, I say, all right guys, if we hit our goal as a gym, what do you guys want? And they'll be like, oh, we want new barbells, we want new, whatever. We want to go out for a team event, we want to go do this or do that. I always let them pick as long as it's reasonable. I usually say $1,000 or less, because they'll work really hard and give you a much higher return on that $1,000 to get whatever prize you're gonna give them. So then this total is just a collective of everybody's performance, right? So what I would do is I would screenshot this every time I feel like there's a meaningful update to it, and then I would send it to my whole team with like a big rally cry of, John just absolutely crushed it. He hit a $2,000 paid in full. He's dominating, he's at 80%. Let's keep it moving, guys. We have two weeks left of the month. Let's absolutely crush it. Get those opportunities in. There's a lot of new challengers here. They totally want to work with you guys one-on-one, but you just have to have the conversation. Like they're not gonna approach you, you have to go approach them. And then your trainers are getting super proactive. They're talking to challengers, they're ascending them to higher levels of service. They're doing all sorts of cool stuff, and it builds a lot of camaraderie, and it starts with this scoreboard. It's you keeping them accountable, you identifying the people that are best for your business, and really keeping them motivated, but you have to stay up to date on this, or they're not gonna care. You have to be super motivating, or they're not gonna care. But if you do this really well, it can be a super powerful tool to getting your team fired up. What I like to do as well is I have a huge whiteboard in our office area, in our team office area, not customers can't see it, only our team can see it, and it's the exact same thing as this. And I have our front desk person, they have access to our sheets, and they update it every day. And so the trainers come in, they have their break, maybe they put something in a locker, they're sitting down, and they can see exactly where everybody stands. And we update it on a daily basis, and it's really, really cool. So this is a really awesome, useful scoreboard to motivate your team if you use it right, give them goals, have them reach those goals, make them maintain their standards, make them do a lot of sessions, make them do sales, make them go out there and get their own opportunities. I've had trainers that got a lot of people in to consultations, even outside of the gym, they just marketed themselves and brought people in the gym to hit their bonuses and their metrics. So again, this is a cool daily scoreboard, and hopefully you guys can find this useful and start crushing it.
